review_scope_note: Time-boxed review (≤25 reads / ≤40 tools). Audit focused on highest-leverage Class TN, HD, IA, CI risks named in the bundle anchor list. Full sentence-walk deferred to round 2 after BLOCKERs are resolved; this round triages structural integrity of Lean-theorem name claims and cross-bundle/registry references.

sentences.agent_notes: TN+HD BLOCKER: The entire §6 (Doublon-SWAP gate) cites a Lean module SKEFTHawking.DoublonGate that does NOT EXIST in lean_deps.json (10330 declarations indexed). No file lean/SKEFTHawking/DoublonGate.lean is present (find -iname '*doublon*' returns nothing). All three theorem names referenced (berryPhase_eq_pi, H_AdiabaticDoublonDrag, H_AdiabaticDoublonDrag_witness_iff_berry_phase_pi) and the tracked-Prop H_AdiabaticDoublonDrag are absent. The corresponding hypothesis is also NOT in HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY (which contains 7 entries: rokhlin_sigma_mod_16, modular_invariance_framing, c_minus_equals_8Nf, characteristic_square_mod_8, spin_bordism_iso_Z, H_KLRS_SM_Crossover, H_ScalarChannelIsTetradBifurcationOutput). Paper18 (the source for §6 per source_manifest.md) appears to have NOT been formalised in Lean at the doublon-gate level — only FermiHubbardDimer.lean exists. The paper claims this as 'the substrate's first symmetry-protected topological gate' with three formalised theorems, but none of them exist. Either the section must be marked openly as un-formalised or the formalisation must be produced.

sentences.quote: H_HorizonBoundaryCondition is the principal tracked Prop ... The same Prop reappears here as the RT/Casini--Huerta knife-edge anchor.
sentences.quote_normalized: h_horizonboundarycondition is the principal tracked prop the same prop reappears here as the rt/casini--huerta knife-edge anchor
sentences.agent_notes: TN: Both §1 abstract and §8 attribute H_HorizonBoundaryCondition to the RTCasiniHuertaBounds module, but the actual Lean structure SKEFTHawking.BHEntropyMicroscopic.H_HorizonBoundaryCondition lives in BHEntropyMicroscopic (a D3 module). lean_deps.json shows the only RTCasiniHuertaBounds tracked Props are H_CasiniHuerta_Bound_Valid and H_RT_Formula_Valid. The cross-bundle attribution must be corrected — D4 §8 should say 'the same H_HorizonBoundaryCondition Prop from D3's BHEntropyMicroscopic module', not present it as locally defined in RTCasiniHuertaBounds. HD: H_HorizonBoundaryCondition is also NOT in HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY (7 entries; this name absent), so its disclosure status as a 'tracked-hypothesis Prop' is registry-orphan; either the registry must be extended or the paper must clarify it is a local Lean structure (not a project-wide tracked Prop with eliminability metadata).

TN advisory (not a hard BLOCKER): The paper writes theorem names as 'QECHolographyBridge.<name>' but the Lean qualified names are SKEFTHawking.QECHolographyBridge.HPCode.<name> (the inner HPCode namespace is omitted in the paper). Theorems exist (verified in QECHolographyBridge.lean lines 100, 125, 142, 167, 208, 249, 265, 297, 326, 371). Recommendation: either include the HPCode namespace in the LaTeX (\texttt{QECHolographyBridge.HPCode.code_distance_...}), or open the HPCode namespace at the start of QECHolographyBridge and re-prove these as top-level aliases. Otherwise readers grep'ing for the names will get false-negative 'theorem not found' answers. Same comment applies to QECHolographyBridge.fibonacci_HPCode_* and QECHolographyBridge.trivialAbelian_violates_admissibility — these are at SKEFTHawking.QECHolographyBridge.<name> (top-level, ok) but the HPCode-namespaced ones aren't.

sentences.quote: Topological quantum computation~\cite{Kitaev2003,FreedmanLarsenWang2002,NayakSimonSternFreedmanDasSarma2008} relies on three pillars
sentences.quote_normalized: topological quantum computation relies on three pillars
sentences.agent_notes: CI BLOCKER: 4 of 6 citation keys NOT in CITATION_REGISTRY: FreedmanLarsenWang2002, NayakSimonSternFreedmanDasSarma2008, ReshetikhinTuraev1991, Witten1989. Only Kitaev2003 and Roehm2026Strategy resolve. The paper will fail validate.py CHECK 1 (citation_integrity) on submission. Add bibitems to src/core/citations.py CITATION_REGISTRY with arXiv IDs / DOIs (Kitaev2003 quant-ph/0506072 form; Witten 1989 CMP 121:351; Reshetikhin-Turaev 1991 Inv. Math. 103:547; Freedman-Larsen-Wang 2002 CMP 227:605; Nayak et al. 2008 RMP 80:1083). Paper15 / paper14 / paper16_wrt_tqft / paper11 source papers may already cache these — confirm via grep across src/core/citations.py before adding.

sentences.quote: Sixteen of the substrate's load-bearing claims here ship with Lean~4 modules at zero \texttt{sorry}; one tracked-hypothesis bundle (\texttt{H\_HorizonBoundaryCondition}) carries the boundary-condition choice; one true axiom carries over from Bundle~D2 (the 4+1D gapped-interface conjecture).
sentences.quote_normalized: sixteen of the substrate's load-bearing claims here ship with lean 4 modules at zero sorry one tracked-hypothesis bundle h_horizonboundarycondition carries the boundary-condition choice one true axiom carries over from bundle d2
sentences.agent_notes: IA advisory: Abstract claims 'Sixteen of the substrate's load-bearing claims here ship with Lean 4 modules at zero sorry' AND §9.7 says 'sixteen substantive claims close at zero sorry, one tracked-hypothesis bundle ... and one tracked-hypothesis premise'. The 'sixteen' is not enumerated in any one place; manually counting the unique theorem names cited by the paper (Uqsl2 ~5, IsingBraiding ~14, WRTInvariant ~4, QECHolographyBridge ~9, RTCasiniHuertaBounds ~3, plus Drinfeld* ~5) gives ~40 named theorems referenced. The 'sixteen' figure may be 'load-bearing' rather than 'all named'; either (a) make the count traceable to a list/table, or (b) rephrase to 'all load-bearing claims'. Also the §6 doublon claims are part of the asserted '16' but do NOT exist in Lean (see TN BLOCKER) — the count cannot be honoured if those proofs are missing.

All five Uqsl2 Drinfeld-Jimbo relation theorems resolve. They live at SKEFTHawking.uq_<name> (top-level, no Uqsl2 sub-namespace). Paper writes them un-namespaced via \texttt{...}, which is fine. Module Uqsl2.lean confirmed present in lean_deps.json with 35 declarations. PASS.

sentences.quote: R1_sigma, Rpsi_sigma, theta_sigma, theta_psi, R_product, R1_sq, Rpsi_sq, hexagon_I/II/III/pss_psi/pss_one/spp, ribbon_ss_1/ss_psi/sp_sigma/pp_1, gauss_sum, quantum_trace_R3, trefoil_eq_neg_sqrt2, hopf_link_matches_S_matrix
sentences.agent_notes: All 21 IsingBraiding names referenced in §3 resolve in lean_deps.json under SKEFTHawking.IsingBraiding.<name> (or top-level SKEFTHawking.<name>). Includes R-matrix entries, all 6 hexagons, all 4 ribbons, gauss_sum, quantum_trace_R3, trefoil_eq_neg_sqrt2, hopf_link_matches_S_matrix. PASS.

All 4 WRTInvariant names resolve in lean_deps.json (top-level SKEFTHawking namespace, module SKEFTHawking.WRTInvariant). The paper writes them as 'WRTInvariant.<name>' which is the module path; the actual qualified Lean name is just SKEFTHawking.<name>. The references are unambiguous and resolve. PASS. (Minor labeling style: same as QEC TN advisory; paper could prepend 'SKEFTHawking.' or note that WRTInvariant is the module not the namespace.)

sentences.quote: RTCasiniHuertaBounds.rt_entropy_pos discharges positivity; rt_kaulMajumdar_gap_at_reduced_area_two closes the Kaul--Majumdar matching at the reduced-area-2 critical point.
sentences.agent_notes: Three core RT/CH theorems verified in lean_deps.json under SKEFTHawking.RTCasiniHuertaBounds: rt_entropy_pos, rt_kaulMajumdar_gap_at_reduced_area_two, rt_eq_kaulMajumdar_iff_trivial_reduced_area. The knife-edge biconditional structure is confirmed. PASS for these three names.

sentences.quote: S 7 cross-bridge to Bundle D3 S 9 (QEC-bridge canonical form; D3 S 9 sets framing, D4 S 7 ships content). S 8 cross-bridge to Bundle D3 S 7 (same H_HorizonBoundaryCondition Prop carries the abelian-MTC-falsifier knife-edge).
sentences.quote_normalized: cross-bundle bridges to d2 d3 sections
sentences.agent_notes: BCM (cross-bundle consistency advisory): D4 makes four explicit cross-bridge promises (D2 S 3.4 Drinfeld centre; D3 S 7 BH-entropy MTC; D3 S 9 QEC bridge; D3 S 7 RT/CH knife-edge). Verification: D2 S 3.4 (Drinfeld centre) PRESENT in D2 paper_draft.tex, mentions toric-code MTC consistently. D3 paper draft mentions 'Hayden' (6x) and 'Preskill' (7x), confirming D3 S 9 QEC bridge alignment. HOWEVER: D3 paper draft has 0 occurrences of 'Casini', 'Ryu', 'knife-edge', 'rt_eq_kaulMajumdar', 'abelian_MTC_falsifies', or 'H_HorizonBoundaryCondition'. The D4 S 8 cross-bridge to D3 S 7 (RT/CH knife-edge) is one-sided: D4 references D3 but D3 has no return reference. Either D3 needs a sub-section that picks up the abelian-MTC-falsifier (the BHEntropyMicroscopic.abelian_MTC_falsifies_H_HorizonBoundaryCondition theorem genuinely lives in D3's content area) or D4 should soften the claim to 'D4 S 8 builds on the D3 BHEntropyMicroscopic falsifier set'. Advisory blocker — not a hard fail because the underlying Lean object exists, but the bundle-level prose mismatch will trip the bundle_readiness cross-bridge check.

sentences.quote: D4 inherits Bundle~D2's single true axiom \texttt{gapped\_interface\_axiom}
sentences.quote_normalized: d4 inherits bundle d2's single true axiom gapped_interface_axiom
sentences.agent_notes: Class IA spot-check: project-wide axiom count = 1 per CLAUDE.md+memory ('1 axiom'). The paper claims 'single true axiom gapped_interface_axiom'. The axiom name is plausible but not verified in this round; lookup in lean_deps.json was not performed for axiom-kind decls. Recommendation in round 2: grep 'axiom ' lean/SKEFTHawking/*.lean to confirm the exact axiom name + module location, then ensure D4 attribution to D2 matches ARCHITECTURE_SCOPE.md or the D2 paper.

summary.agent_text: 11 spot-checks | 4 PASS | 3 FAIL | 4 WARN | 6 BLOCKERs across the bundle (3 hard FAIL + 3 advisory WARN BCM). Round 1 triages the highest-leverage TN/HD/CI risks named in the bundle anchor list. A full sentence-walk is deferred to round 2 once the BLOCKERs are addressed.

blocking_issues.summary: Class TN BLOCKER: Entire S 6 (Doublon-SWAP gate) cites Lean module DoublonGate that does NOT exist. No DoublonGate.lean file present; lean_deps.json (10330 entries) has zero matches for Doublon, berryPhase, or H_AdiabaticDoublonDrag.
blocking_issues.fix: Either (a) drop S 6 to an open-task / future-work paragraph, or (b) produce lean/SKEFTHawking/DoublonGate.lean before submission with the three named theorems and add H_AdiabaticDoublonDrag to HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY in src/core/constants.py.

blocking_issues.summary: Class TN BLOCKER: H_HorizonBoundaryCondition module attribution wrong. Paper S 8 says it lives in RTCasiniHuertaBounds; actual location is BHEntropyMicroscopic.H_HorizonBoundaryCondition (a D3 module).

Confirm the abelian-MTC-falsifier reference in S 7.5 also points to BHEntropyMicroscopic.abelian_MTC_falsifies_H_HorizonBoundaryCondition (it does in lean_deps.json).

Math. 103:547; Witten 1989 CMP 121:351). Then regenerate citation cache. Spot-check that paper14 / paper16_wrt_tqft / paper11 source papers may have already added these.

blocking_issues.summary: Class HD advisory: H_HorizonBoundaryCondition + H_AdiabaticDoublonDrag are paper-asserted as 'tracked-hypothesis Props' but neither is in HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY (which has only 7 entries: rokhlin_sigma_mod_16, modular_invariance_framing, c_minus_equals_8Nf, characteristic_square_mod_8, spin_bordism_iso_Z, H_KLRS_SM_Crossover, H_ScalarChannelIsTetradBifurcationOutput).
blocking_issues.fix: Either (a) add both Props to HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY with eliminability metadata, or (b) clarify in the paper that they are local Lean structures (not project-wide tracked Props with eliminability records). Note: H_HorizonBoundaryCondition exists structurally in BHEntropyMicroscopic.lean (as a structure, not a Prop in the registry sense).

blocking_issues.summary: Class TN advisory: QECHolographyBridge theorems referenced via 'QECHolographyBridge.<name>' but actual qualified names are 'SKEFTHawking.QECHolographyBridge.HPCode.<name>' (HPCode sub-namespace omitted).

blocking_issues.summary: Class BCM advisory: D4 promises a S 8 cross-bridge to D3 S 7 (RT/CH knife-edge), but D3's paper_draft.tex has 0 mentions of 'Casini', 'Ryu', 'knife-edge', 'rt_eq_kaulMajumdar', 'abelian_MTC_falsifies', or 'H_HorizonBoundaryCondition'. Cross-bridge prose is one-sided.
blocking_issues.fix: Either add a brief paragraph to D3 S 7 acknowledging the BHEntropyMicroscopic.abelian_MTC_falsifies_H_HorizonBoundaryCondition / RT-CH knife-edge connection (and pointing at D4 S 8), or soften D4 S 8's cross-bridge prose from 'cross-bridge' (implying bidirectional) to 'D4 S 8 builds on D3 BHEntropyMicroscopic'. Bundle_readiness cross-bridge cluster check will fail otherwise.

non_blocking_followups.summary: Class IA advisory: 'Sixteen of the substrate's load-bearing claims' count is not enumerated. Round 2 should produce a table or list naming the 16, especially given that 3 of them (S 6 doublon claims) cannot be honoured per BLOCKER 1.
